wire_magnetism_diagram: The image shows a current-carrying wire perpendicular to the page, a marked point P, and labeled arrow options.
The diagram shows a wire passing into or out of the page, point P nearby, and labeled field-direction arrows.
This figure shows a page-perpendicular wire carrying current, a nearby point P, and four labeled arrow options.
The visual shows a dot or cross current symbol, a marked point P, and arrow choices for the field direction.
The diagram shows a current-carrying wire through the page and direction options for the field at point P.

wire_field_direction_choice_query: Use the right-hand rule around the wire. A dot-in-circle means current out of the page, and a cross-in-circle means current into the page. Choose the arrow option for the field at P.
Apply the right-hand rule to the current symbol and point P. Dot means current out of the page; cross means current into the page. Select the matching arrow.
Use the wire current cue and point P to determine the circular magnetic-field direction. Choose the labeled arrow option.
Use the right-hand rule around the page-perpendicular wire to choose the field direction at P. Match the result to one labeled arrow.
Identify the labeled arrow for the magnetic field at P. Dot is out of page, cross is into page, and the field circles around the wire.

single: Which labeled arrow gives the magnetic-field direction at point P?
Which arrow option shows the magnetic field at P?
What labeled arrow option matches the field direction at point P?
Choose the arrow option for the field direction at P.
Which option gives the magnetic-field direction around the wire at P?
